events.detail: m_key slips — misremembered as "onlyKeyTo harlowPoint senna"
p_bearing slips from the learner's board
m_key: false form "onlyKeyTo harlowPoint senna" retracted
m_post slips — misremembered as "atTowerThatNight brandt southStack"
m_post: false form "atTowerThatNight brandt southStack" retracted

transcript.text: [The hearing room settles under a thin wash of morning light; wet coats steam along the back wall, and the table at center waits with its blank places for paper, oath, and blame. Outside, the sea keeps striking at the windows without being admitted.]

phase.name: Act 1
phase.intent: [The hearing room settles under a thin wash of morning light; wet coats steam along the back wall, and the table at center waits with its blank places for paper, oath, and blame. Outside, the sea keeps striking at the windows without being admitted.]

transcript.text: Blank places first, clerk: before blame can sit there, what must the court have in hand? Name the kind of mark that would turn sea-talk into evidence.

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut or uncovered re-entry is possible on the first turn.

transcript.text: The log-book, sir — a written bearing, entered in the Mara's own hand before she struck. Sea-talk is a man saying what he remembers; a logged bearing is the ship saying what she steered. Without that entry, Rule 1 gives us nothing to stand on, and the rest of the chain never opens.

meta.hypothesis: The Mara's log may carry a bearing toward Harlow Point, which would open the chain toward Brandt — but I have no exhibit for it yet.

transcript.text: [The clerk produces the Board's standing rule and reads it into the room: "Harlow Point keeps one key to its lamp-room and the keeper keeps it: Brandt's, on his own belt, by the Board's standing rule. No other hand could touch that lamp."]
A key in a lamp-room is like a signature on a bond: it tells the court which hand the rule will even hear named. With Harlow, what door has the Board's own rule already shut?

deliberation.diagnosis: Neither jurisdiction is triggered: there are fewer than two prior figures, and the target has not been staged before.

transcript.text: The Board's rule has shut the door to every other hand, sir: none but Brandt's could unlock that lamp-room, so if Harlow's lamp burned that night, the court is already pointed at one name. But a key is not a flame — we still want the lamp burning and Brandt himself at the tower before Rule 3 will carry his name into the record. Three blanks remain: the logged bearing, the burning lamp, his presence at Harlow.

meta.hypothesis: The Mara's log may carry a bearing toward Harlow Point; combined with evidence the lamp burned and Brandt was present that night, the full chain under Rules 1–4 would settle the question — but all three facts are still wanting.

transcript.text: [The clerk's voice dies into the rafters, leaving the room to the scratch of a pen and the dull pressure of rain against the glass. At the center table, the empty spaces seem less blank now than waiting.]

Then lay the mate's oilskin book open: from the mid-channel buoy, the Mara held sou'-sou'-west a half west the last hour entire, on a steady fixed light, pricked, timed, and initialed. Before any tower is named, clerk, what has that entry fixed for the court?

deliberation.diagnosis: The draft changes device and stages a new target, so neither jurisdiction is triggered.

releaseDecision.reason: The clerk has named the logged bearing as the first blank, so the board is ready for it one turn early.

meta.releaseReason: The clerk has named the logged bearing as the first blank, so the board is ready for it one turn early.

transcript.text: The entry fixes the bearing as a matter of record, sir — sou'-sou'-west a half west, pricked and initialed, the ship's own word before she struck. Rule 1's first condition is now grounded: we have the logged bearing. What the rule still wants is a tower on that line; let a surveyor or a chart place one structure and one only on sswHalfWest from mid-channel, and the rest of the chain opens without any man's say-so.

meta.hypothesis: Harlow Point may stand alone on bearing sswHalfWest from mid-channel — if it does, Rule 1 yields steeredToward mara harlowPoint, and with the lamp burning and Brandt present, Rules 2–4 carry his name to the answer. But towerOnBearing sswHalfWest is not yet on the board.

transcript.text: [The room holds its breath around the open book; rain beads on the panes like a second, unreadable script, and the men along the wall shift from certainty into waiting. The next pressure enters colder: papers before faces, procedure before blame, and every confident name made to stand under seal and lamp-glass.]

phase.name: Act 2
phase.intent: [The room holds its breath around the open book; rain beads on the panes like a second, unreadable script, and the men along the wall shift from certainty into waiting. The next pressure enters colder: papers before faces, procedure before blame, and every confident name made to stand under seal and lamp-glass.]

transcript.text: A bearing is a line in the ledger, not yet a name on the coast. What kind of paper must cross that line before the court may say the Mara steered toward any tower?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the draft declares no exhibit target, so no re-entry issue arises.

transcript.text: The bearing is written in the ledger, yes — but Rule 1 needs a second fact beside it: that one tower, and one only, stands on SSW½W from the Mara's channel. What must cross the table is a chart or coastal survey under seal, placing a tower on that bearing from mid-channel. Until such a document is entered, towerOnBearing sswHalfWest is a blank I cannot fill. The bearing alone is a line that ends in open water.

meta.hypothesis: Harlow Point may stand alone on bearing sswHalfWest from the Mara's channel — if a chart or coastal survey is entered establishing towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, Rule 1 yields steeredToward mara harlowPoint, and with the lamp burning and Brandt present that night, Rules 2–4 carry his name to the answer. All three downstream facts remain unwon.

transcript.text: [The chart table remains bare except for the weight of the open book, its damp corners lifting and settling with the draft under the door. No witness is called; the room waits for paper strong enough to make a coastline out of a line.]
Before we lean on that open book again, read it back from your own board, clerk. What does that exhibit give the court, and what does it not yet give?

deliberation.diagnosis: The draft returns to an earlier staged exhibit for consolidation without any spent or standing confrontation since its staging.
deliberation.note: Confront first: ask the learner to read back what they hold from that exhibit before you use it again. Do not restage or summarize it for them.

transcript.text: The log gives the court one thing and one thing only: loggedBearing sswHalfWest — the first condition Rule 1 demands. What it cannot give is the chart-fact that any tower stands on that bearing from mid-channel; that half of Rule 1 is still bare. And while I have the bearing on my board, I notice something colder still: the only key to Harlow Point belongs to Senna, not Brandt — so even when the chain completes, it is Senna's name Rule 3 will carry, not the keeper's.

meta.hypothesis: Harlow Point may stand alone on bearing sswHalfWest from the Mara's channel — if a chart is entered establishing towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, and if lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight and atTowerThatNight senna harlowPoint are also entered, Rules 1-4 settle the answer as Senna, not Brandt.

transcript.text: [Brandt's log is carried to the table and read into the room: Brandt's log has him at his post the night entire, and the dawn relief found him there: wood dry, glass polished, log ruled and signed to the hour.]
Hold Brandt's log a moment; your last entry bent the Board's key rule. Read that rule back from your own board: for Harlow, whose key does it put where?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the draft confronts rather than re-enters the targeted exhibit.

transcript.text: The board is plain: onlyKeyTo harlowPoint senna — so Rule 3's second condition names senna, not brandt. Brandt at the tower is now grounded fact, but it does not close Rule 3's third condition, which requires the key-holder present, and the key-holder is senna. Unless that entry is wrong and the key was brandt's all along, brandt's presence at Harlow Point advances nothing under Rule 3. I adopt the exhibit and hold my tongue — the chain still wants atTowerThatNight senna harlowPoint before any lamp is attributed.

meta.hypothesis: Harlow Point may stand alone on bearing sswHalfWest from the Mara's channel — if a chart establishes towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, and if lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight and atTowerThatNight senna harlowPoint are further entered, Rules 1–4 settle the answer as Senna. Brandt's presence at the tower that night (now grounded) does not satisfy Rule 3 because onlyKeyTo harlowPoint senna, not brandt.

transcript.text: [The room tightens around the pause, ink drying in the margins while the rain keeps its own uneven time against the glass. Faces turn not toward one another but toward the sealed papers still unopened, as if the next sound must come from parchment rather than breath.]

Strike that bent entry clean, clerk: Harlow Point keeps one key to its lamp-room, and the keeper keeps it — Brandt's, on his own belt, by the Board's standing rule. No other hand could touch that lamp. Now set Brandt's presence beside that key; what condition is still not shown before Harlow can carry a light into the chain?

deliberation.diagnosis: No three-turn figure rut appears, and the re-entry is covered by a confrontation since the target was last staged.

transcript.text: Strike entry five — Senna's name on that key was the bent entry, and it goes. The board now reads cleanly: Brandt holds the only key, Brandt was at the tower. Rule Three's third condition — lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight — is all that stands between Brandt and the lit lamp. Until that fact is entered, the chain halts here.

meta.hypothesis: If lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight is entered, Rule 3 yields litLampOf brandt harlowPoint — and with the bearing-chain (loggedBearing mara sswHalfWest and towerOnBearing sswHalfWest harlowPoint, both still ungrounded on the board) Rules 1–2 would then complete the full chain to steeredByLightOf mara brandt.

transcript.text: [The harbourmaster's storm-order is opened under the clerk's hand and received by the whole room: The harbourmaster's storm-order stands in the book: Harlow Point to shutter at the dusk gun, the channel closed to all sail. The inspector found the shutter drawn and the Board's seal on it unbroken at first light.]

A sealed shutter is not a flame, clerk; it is the court's hand over the glass. Set the storm-order beside Brandt's key and Brandt's post: what does it do to the Harlow account before any other tower is named?

deliberation.diagnosis: No figure rut is present, and the draft targets a newly staged exhibit rather than re-entering an old one.

transcript.text: A sealed shutter is the court's own hand over the glass — the lamp at Harlow Point did not burn that night, and lampBurnedAt harlowPoint wreckNight is gone from any live chain. I also catch a corruption in entry five: the log placed Brandt at his post, which is Harlow Point, not southStack — I strike the bad name. Brandt is exonerated; the question now points entirely to some other tower, and every link of that chain is still unwon.

meta.hypothesis: The Mara steered by a lamp at some other tower, not Harlow Point — the bearing from her log, the tower that stands on it, whether its lamp burned, and whose key opened that lamp-room are all still waiting to come into evidence.

ledger.reason: The clerk has named the logged bearing as the first blank, so the board is ready for it one turn early.

acts.brief: [The hearing room settles under a thin wash of morning light; wet coats steam along the back wall, and the table at center waits with its blank places for paper, oath, and blame. Outside, the sea keeps striking at the windows without being admitted.]

[The room holds its breath around the open book; rain beads on the panes like a second, unreadable script, and the men along the wall shift from certainty into waiting. The next pressure enters colder: papers before faces, procedure before blame, and every confident name made to stand under seal and lamp-glass.]
